head.text: Heavy, dull, confused. Headache with least exertion

generalities.text: [Mental] Irritable, sensitive to noise. Mental fatigue, nausea and vertigo, worse in a closed room

ears.text: Roaring and ringing; voice sounds strange; ears seem to close up suddenly

stomach_abdomen.text: Pain in bowels; relieved by stool. Desire for stool immediately after eating. Dysentery

female.text: Dysmenorrhœa, with bearing-down pains, tenderness, drawing in groins. Menses suppressed; later, profuse

respiratory.text: Hurried, labored, stertorous respiration. Frothy mucus obstructs the air-passages

keynotes.text: color="#800000">TANACETUM VULGARE Tansy Abnormal lassitude. Nervous and tired feeling. "Half dead, half alive feeling" all over. Of use in chorea and reflex spasms (worms). Said to be a specific against effects of poison ivy.

Text Book of Materia Medica. By Adolph Lippe, M. D. Professor of Materia Medica at the Homopathic College of Pennsylvania. Presented by Médi-T. Tanacetum vulgare. Great mobility ; extraordinary motion, and strange gesticulations. St. Vitus's dance. Copyright © Médi-T 2006
